title: Astracantha psilacmos
taxonomy: Astracantha psilacmos is a species of spiny shrub in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the Middle East and is a member of the genus Astracantha. Astracantha psilacmos is also known as the thorny pea or the spiny pea. The species is classified under the subfamily Faboideae and the family Fabaceae. Further classification can be broken down to kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Astracantha psilacmos, also known as a type of Astragalus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha psilacmos include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Astracantha psilacmos is commonly prepared in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
